title: wolftpm-tss: null-byte programmer error silently maps to Error::Transport{code:-1}
description: Scenario: WolfTpmSwtpm::connect(host, port) returns Error::Transport { code: -1 } if host contains a null byte. The code -1 is neither a wolfTPM internal error code (those are large negative i32 like -173) nor a TPM2 spec code (positive). A caller that pattern-matches Error::Transport cannot distinguish 'programmer passed a bad host string' from 'the transport returned -1'. The comment acknowledges 'wolftpm-tss has no InvalidArg variant' but does not offer a path forward. Maintenance consequence: since Error is #[non_exhaustive], a dedicated InvalidArg variant can be added without breaking downstream matches. Users who file an issue about misleading errors cannot easily understand the root cause. Fix: add an InvalidArg(&'static str) variant to wolftpm_tss::Error (non_exhaustive allows this without a semver break) and use it in WolfTpmSwtpm::connect.
